title: Wednesday's top stories in 10 minutes
summary: G7 finance ministers work to keep relations friendly at summit in Banff, Alberta.    Quebec's culture minister to table bill that will force digital platforms to add more francophone content.   Former Israeli Prime Minister Ehud Olmert says allies should keep up pressure on Israel to lift aid blockade on Gaza.  Leaders in Puvirnituq, Quebec ask Ottawa for help with local water crisis.   Far-right Romanian presidential candidate George Simion says he will challenge election results.   South African President Cyril Ramaphosa to meet U.S. President Donald Trump at White House.   Some Canadian 2SLGBTQ+ athletes skipping World Pride in Washington, D.C., citing political climate in U.S.

title: CTV National News for May 20: How a W5 investigation led to the arrest of a Hamilton, Ont. man
summary: A Hamilton man was arrested in connection to a W5 investigation into men sharing video allegedly drugging and assaulting women; Canada, the U.K. and France have called the level of human suffering in Gaza 'intolerable' amid an 11-week aid blockade by Israel.

title: ពត៌មានប្រចាំថ្ងៃនៃសារព័ត៌មាន ឌឹ ខេមបូឌា ដេលីសម្រាប់រាត្រីថ្ងៃទី២០ ខែឧសភា ឆ្នាំ២០២៥
podcast_name: ព័ត៌មានប្រចាំថ្ងៃ - ឌឹ ខេមបូឌា ដេលី
summary: ១) ក្រសួងការងារអំពាវនាវឱ្យ កម្មកររោងចក្រ ខល័រស្តារ (Color Star) រង់ចាំការដោះស្រាយរបស់ក្រសួងជាមួយក្រុមហ៊ុន។ ២) បក្សក្រៅរដ្ឋាភិបាលជំរុញឱ្យ លោក ហ៊ុន ម៉ាណែត បញ្ជូន​អនុសញ្ញា​អង្គការសហប្រជាជាតិស្តីពីច្បាប់សមុទ្រទៅរដ្ឋសភាផ្តល់​សច្ចាប័នឱ្យបានឆាប់។ ៣) ​លោក ហ៊ុន សែន៖ ដើម្បីកុំឱ្យរបបប្រល័យ ពូជសាសន៍វិល​ត្រឡប់មកវិញពលរដ្ឋខ្មែរគ្រប់រូបត្រូវចូលរួមថែរក្សាការពារសន្តិភាព។ ៤) មជ្ឈមណ្ឌល​សម្រាប់ការ​សិក្សា​អន្តរជាតិ​និងយុទ្ធសាស្ត្រ​របស់​អាមេរិករកឃើញថា៖ ចិនកំពុងកាន់កាប់ផ្តាច់​មុខ​កន្លែងខ្លះនៃមូលដ្ឋាន​កងទ័ព​ជើង​ទឹក​សមុទ្ររាមរបស់ខ្មែរ។ ​ ៥) ពូទីននៅតែបន្តច្រានចោលបទឈប់បាញ់បើអ៊ុយក្រែនមិន​ព្រមដក​ទ័ពចេញពី​តំបន់ដែលរុស្ស៊ីកាន់កាប់។

title: Full Show May 20th, 2025:  Canada’s New Housing Minister Says Prices Shouldn't Fall, Canada Post Strike, And Coal Mining Project Approval Threatens Alberta’s Water Future
summary: TOPIC 1: Liberals Promise Housing Affordability, But Minister Says Prices Shouldn’t Fall  GUEST 1: Dr. Alexandra Flynn, Associate Professor Peter A. Allard School of Law At UBC and Director, Housing Research Collaborative TOPIC 2: Canada Post receives strike notices from CUPW  GUEST 2: Marvin Ryder, associate professor at McMaster University DeGroote school of business TOPIC 3: Grassy Mountain Approval Threatens Alberta’s Water Future says Environmental Advocate  GUEST 3: Stephen Legault, Senior Manager at Alberta Energy Transition

title: Another Canada Post strike is set. Is there any hope for a deal?
summary: Canada Post's Jon Hamilton and the Canadian Union of Postal Workers' Jim Gallant lay out the barriers still preventing a deal as they count down to a strike planned for Friday. As G7 finance ministers arrive in Alberta, six-time representative of the prime minister for the summit Peter Boehm breaks down how the Trump administration's overturning of the global trade order will shake up this week's meetings. Plus, NDP MP Heather McPherson defends interim leader Don Davies after three of the party's seven MPs disputed his appointment.

title: Mounting criticism for Israel, Canada’s increasing food costs, the momentum behind the PWHL’s popularity, and more
summary: Israel faces increasing international pressure over its war against Hamas amid new strikes, and little aid distribution in Gaza. Canada, Britain, and France are threatening action if Israel does not end its military offensive and lift blockades. Also: The latest economic snapshot for Canada shows the price at the pumps was down, but the cost at the checkout counters keeps going up – with things like beef, and coffee and tea seeing eye-watering increases. And: The soaring popularity of the Professional Women’s Hockey League, as full-throated fans get set for the championship series between Ottawa and Minnesota. Plus: President Trump’s ‘Golden Dome’ missile defence system; CBC News is in Iran, speaking with women daring to break the rules by ditching their hijabs; Canada House’s century celebration in the U.K., and more.

title: REBEL ROUNDUP | Feds push MAID for teens, Carney defends housing prices, Canada's fent problem
summary: Today, we're looking at an assisted suicide program being promoted to teenagers and the social harms from Canada's shameful Medical Assistance in Dying program. Plus, Prime Minister Mark Carney is defending Canada's outrageous housing prices and his government's failure to release a budget. And finally, FBI Director Kash Patel called out the fentanyl crisis in Canada, with the country becoming a hotbed for drug trafficking. Special Guest: Lise Merle.

title: Canada Post receives strike notice from the union representing postal workers for the second time in six months
summary: CUPW negotiator, Jim Gallant joins Vassy to break down key issues in the dispute and chances of reaching a deal with Canada Post.  On today's show:   Eric Ham, CTV News U.S Political Analyst on Former U.S President Joe Biden’s cancer diagnosis Pedro Antunes, Chief economist at the Conference Board of Canada breaks down key factors that influenced Canada’s inflation rate which dropped to 1.7 per cent in April. Robyn Doolittle, reporter with the Globe and Mail on the latest developments in the Hockey Canada sexual assault trial The Daily Debrief Panel with Laryssa Waler,  Founder of Henley Strategies and former head of communications for Doug Ford, Nojoud Al mallees, economics reporter for the Globe  and Mail and Sharan Kaur, partner with Sovereign Advisory CUPW negotiator, Jim Gallant talks about the potential of another Canada Post strike.

title: Sean Speer on Mark Carney's Missed Opportunity
summary: Canada's ramping up pressure on Israel alongside France and the UK. Mark Carney's new cabinet is sworn in, and the PM's got less than six weeks to fulfill his promise of "free trade in Canada before Canada Day". And, the Toronto Maple Leafs will have to wait (yet) another year for a shot at Lord Stanley's Cup. We catch up on the top stories in Canada with The Hub's editor at large Sean Speer (3:00). THE HUB: https://thehub.ca/2025/05/15/sean-speer-the-maple-leafs-playoff-woes-are-a-microcosm-of-a-bigger-set-of-canadian-problems/ JOIN SEAN & RYAN at the EDMONTON REAL ESTATE FORUM on MAY 27: https://informaconnect.com/edmonton-real-estate-forum/ 31:30 | Jespo and Johnny catch up after the May Long weekend, including the heartbreaking *and* heartwarming story involving Jets' star Mark Scheifele, and the Oilers' date with Dallas.  54:00 | Add another layer to the UCP/AHS story. title: Canada will be the last stronghold in the scandal of gender medicine
summary: Guests and Topics: -Trump says Russia-Ukraine ceasefire talks will begin immediately following call with Putin with Guest: Adam Zivo, National Post columnist and Executive Director for the Centre For Responsible Drug Policy -Canada will be the last stronghold in the scandal of gender medicine with Guest: Mia Hughes, Senior fellow at the Macdonald-Laurier Institute. Specializes in researching pediatric gender medicine, psychiatric epidemics, social contagion and the intersection of trans rights and women’s rights If you enjoyed the podcast, tell a friend! title: Ép. 20/05 | Sommes-nous de moins en moins tolérants?
summary: Les Québécois ne veulent plus rien savoir de la taxe carbone | Mark Carney réussit là où Pierre Poilievre à échoué | Vers une autre grève chez Postes Canada? | Ça coûte cher de remplacer une porte au CHUM… | La FIQ dénonce les compressions du gouvernement |   Sophie Thibault sera honorée à l’Assemblée nationale ce mardi  Dans cet épisode intégral du 20 mai, en entrevue :    Yannick Scott, directeur national pour la région du Montréal métropolitain pour le Syndicat des travailleurs et travailleuses des postes. Julie Bouchard, présidente de la Fédération interprofessionnelle de la santé du Québec (FIQ). Éric Yvan Lemay, journaliste au Bureau d’enquête de Québecor. Sophie Thibault, cheffe d’antenne du TVA Nouvelles.      Une production QUB  Mai 2025 Pour de l’information concernant l’utilisation de vos données personnelles - https://omnystudio.com/policies/listener/fr

title: Harcelée par un homme avec des troubles mentaux: «On dirait que la non-responsabilité criminelle devient une facilité»
summary: Écoutez l'essentiel de l'émission Lagacé le matin du mardi 20 2025:   Le tour d’horizon de Patrick Lagacé: «56 % des Québécois veulent la fin de la taxe carbone provinciale» Harcelée par un homme avec des troubles mentaux: «On dirait que la non-responsabilité criminelle devient une facilité» Déclaration de l’ex-président de la SAAQ: «On est dégoûtés» -Antoine Bittar Préparations pour nourrisson: «50$ par semaine, c'est non négligeable» Joe Biden: «Beaucoup de questions et même des théories du complot» Aînés: «La maltraitance, c'est inacceptable dans une transaction immobilière» Musique, humour et séparation: «C'était difficile parce que les gens ne savent pas ce qu'on a vécu» -Marc Dupré Voir https://www.cogecomedia.com/vie-privee pour notre politique de vie privée

title: Les Québécois ne veulent plus de la taxe carbone... Y'EN ONT DÉJÀ VOULU???
summary: Le grand journal de Maurais live

title: Foreign leaders are already lavishing PM Carney with costly gifts
summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s barely a month in office and he’s already accepting lavish gifts from foreign leaders. In just 10 years, Canada racked up the largest government spending and debt increase in the G7 — nearly outpacing every advanced country on Earth. Canada is building fewer homes in 2025 than it did last year, and the biggest slowdowns are coming from Ontario and British Columbia. Tune into The Daily Brief with Cosmin Dzsurdzsa and Geoff Knight! Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

title: Tuesday's top stories in 10 minutes
summary: Canada Post workers poised to walk off the job on Friday morning.   Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu calls joint statement from Canada, UK and France a "huge prize" for Hamas.  United Nations humanitarian chief warns 14,000 babies could die if more aid does not reach Gaza in the next 48 hours.   Ontario government buying 2 new helicopters to boost security along the U-S border.   G7 finance officials meet in Banff, ahead of leaders' summit next month.   Researchers say long waits for hip and knee replacements in Canada could be eliminated.    World Health Organization approves global treaty on improving pandemic preparedness.   King Charles and Queen Camilla visit Canada House to mark 100 years since it was established in London.

title: «Ce sont les Américains qui sont les principaux bénéficiaires des fonds religieux qui viennent du Canada»
podcast_name: La revue de presse de Paul Arcand
summary: À l'occasion de la revue de presse, mardi, Valérie Lebeuf, en remplacement de Paul Arcand pour les deux prochaines semaines, fait le point sur les 700 millions de dollars de fonds publics qui ont servi à financer des organismes religieux à l'étranger, tel que rapporté par La Presse.  «Au Canada, 12% des transferts à l'étranger servent à l'avancement de la religion et 9% à celui de l'éducation. Et quand on se demande où va l'argent, ce sont les Américains qui sont les principaux bénéficiaires des fonds religieux qui viennent du Canada, soit 434 millions de dollars depuis maintenant cinq ans.»  Autres sujets abordés   Le conflit à Postes Canada pourrait se poursuivre. Les employés syndiqués prévoient faire à nouveau la grève dès vendredi; Donald Trump croit que des progrès ont été accomplis lors de sa conversation téléphonique avec Vladimir Poutine, mais des experts voient les choses autrement; Les projets de construction et d'agrandissement des écoles mis sur pause pourraient coûter cher au gouvernement du Québec... Voir https://www.cogecomedia.com/vie-privee pour notre politique de vie privée

title: Mark Carney promised affordable housing. Will he deliver?
summary: “Build baby, build” is how Prime Minister Mark Carney pledged to tackle Canada’s housing crisis, but his new housing minister says that won’t mean reducing house prices. Matt Galloway asks housing experts to unpack the new Liberal government’s strategy, and unpick the “Gordian Knot” of whether Canada can create affordable housing without prices dropping?

title: CTV National News for May 19: Trump begins ceasefire talks with Putin, Zelensky
summary: U.S. President Trump has separate ceasefire calls with Vladmir Putin and Volodymyr Zelensky; plus, more Canadians are spending their vacations within the country.

title: ពត៌មានប្រចាំថ្ងៃនៃសារព័ត៌មាន ឌឹ ខេមបូឌា ដេលីសម្រាប់រាត្រីថ្ងៃទី១៩ ខែឧសភា ឆ្នាំ២០២៥
podcast_name: ព័ត៌មានប្រចាំថ្ងៃ - ឌឹ ខេមបូឌា ដេលី
summary: ១) ​​អ្នកឃ្លាំមើល៖បើសមយុទ្ធនាគមាសដើរហួសពី​គោលការណ៍​អាព្យាក្រឹតនឹងនាំទុក្ខដាក់ខ្លួន។ ២) ​ អាជ្ញាធរ​ នឹងបំពាក់បន្ថែមឧបករណ៍ការពាររន្ទះនៅតាម​ប្រាសាទ​បន្ទាប់​ពីមានបាតុភូតរន្ទះបាញ់បណ្ដាលឱ្យមនុស្សស្លាប់។ ៣) ​ បក្សប្រឆាំងស្នើឱ្យ លោក ហ៊ុន ម៉ាណែត ដាក់សំណើទៅរដ្ឋ​សភា​ធ្វើសច្ចាប័នលើច្បាប់សមុទ្រ។ ៤) ​ លោក ហ៊ុន ម៉ាណែតអំពាវនាវឱ្យ ពលរដ្ឋខ្មែរចូលរួម​ថែ​រក្សា​សន្តិភាពដើម្បីកុំឱ្យរបបប្រល័យ ពូជសាសន៍វិលមកវិញ។ ៥) ​ HRW ថា ជប៉ុនមិនត្រូវអោបដៃឈមើល​រដ្ឋាភិបាលកម្ពុជាបន្ត​​កៀបសង្កត់ និងរំលោភ​សិទ្ធិមនុស្សតាមទំនើងចិត្តទៀតនោះទេ។ ៦) ក្រោយពីចរចា​បរាជ័យ ដាណឺម៉ាកផ្តល់ជំនួយយោធា ៦០០លាន​ដុល្លារខណៈអ៊ីតាលីបញ្ជូនរថពាសដែកចំនួន៤០០គ្រឿងទៅឱ្យអ៊ុយក្រែន។

title: The Country Full Show: Tuesday, May 20, 2025
summary: Jamie Mackay talks to Richard McIntyre, Mark de Lautour, Farmer Tom Martin, Kate Acland, and Corina Jordan.  See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: Rebuilding after forest fires, butterfly decline, new Canadian baseball star and more
summary: Fire season has already started in Canada, with several communities having been given evacuation orders. But in British Columbia, some condo owners are still living with the consequences of a fire that was put out years ago. And: With spring and summer taking hold in Canada, it is normally a time to see butterflies fluttering around. But according to a recent study, the numbers of butterflies are dramatically dropping. And a large part of the butterfly population has vanished.  Also: In a league of her own. One of Japan’s top female baseball stars took to the mound for a pro men’s team in Canada. She pitched two shut out innings for the Toronto Maple Leaf Baseball team in her debut.  Plus: child obesity, robotic healthcare, being detained at U.S. border and more.
